description: Indian English L1-influenced phoneme tolerances. Drafted from published IE phonology references (Wells 1982; Sailaja 2009; Gargesh 2004). Word-level forced alignment treats these as 'accent_tolerated' rather than substitutions/omissions. Per-phoneme grading is NOT exposed to students; this ruleset only affects whether a word with low per-phoneme confidence is bumped back to 'correct'.

tolerated_substitutions.note: z often devoiced or merged with s
TRAP vowel often merged toward DRESS or open central
stressed schwa often pronounced as full vowel; syllable-timed
rhoticity varies; non-rhotic acceptable, retroflex tap acceptable
retroflex realization of /t/ â€” common, not an error
occasional merger; less common but documented

tolerated_stress_shifts.description: Stress placement is often L1-influenced and varies widely; word-level scoring should NOT penalise non-target stress placement when phoneme content is otherwise correct.

notes_for_reviewers: This ruleset is intentionally permissive â€” the design philosophy is 'when ambiguous, favour correct'.
Phoneme-level penalties are not applied to student-visible accuracy scores. Words flagged as 'accent_tolerated' count as correct.
Outright wrong words (different word entirely) are still caught by word-level alignment regardless of this ruleset.
Tune by adding rules that the shadow-mode comparison reveals are over-flagged â€” never by removing rules.
